title: acer tegmentosum var. pavolinii
intro: Acer tegmentosum var. pavolinii, also known as Pavlov's maple, is a variety of the Acer tegmentosum tree species. This deciduous tree is primarily native to East Asia, specifically China, Japan, and Korea, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of the Acer tegmentosum var. pavolinii contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system.
